data.title: South Korean Police Officers Accused of Bribery in Cryptocurrency Money Laundering Case
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, two South Korean police officers have been accused of accepting bribes from an illegal cryptocurrency exchange, with the total amount of money laundering involved reaching $1.86 billion (approximately 2.496 trillion Korean won). Prosecutors allege that the officers provided criminal groups with investigative intelligence, assisted in unfreezing accounts, introduced lawyers, and connected them with other law enforcement personnel in exchange for financial compensation. South Korean authorities have frozen related assets worth about $1.1 million (1.5 billion Korean won).

The money laundering operation was orchestrated by a main perpetrator in collaboration with a CEO known as "A." Between January and October 2024, they recruited members to establish cash-to-cryptocurrency exchange points disguised as "gift voucher shops" in bustling areas like Yeoksam-dong, Gangnam District, Seoul. These locations were used to convert proceeds from voice phishing scams into the stablecoin USDT. To avoid suspicion, the shops even displayed warnings against voice phishing scams.

data.title: South Korea tightens crypto regulations again: Crypto "travel rules" expanded to include transactions under 1 million won.
data.description: South Korean Financial Services Commission Chairman Lee Eun-woon stated at the 19th Anti-Money Laundering Day event held by the Financial Intelligence Analysis Institute on the 28th that South Korea will further strengthen anti-money laundering regulations for cryptocurrencies. He pointed out that the "travel rule," known as the "cryptocurrency real-name system," will be expanded to cover small transfers of less than 1 million won (approximately US$680) to close existing regulatory blind spots. Lee emphasized that regulators will severely crack down on money laundering activities using virtual assets and prohibit virtual asset transactions with overseas exchanges with high money laundering risks. Furthermore, South Korea will establish a stricter review system to comprehensively examine the criminal records, financial status, and social credit of virtual asset business operators and their major shareholders to strengthen industry entry barriers. (Yonhap News Agency)

data.title: CME market disruptions impacted some Bybit TradFi indices and commodities.
data.description: It has been reported that a complete disruption to the CME Globex futures and options market has caused underlying market data to stop updating, and all liquidity providers are unable to provide real-time quotes for products such as US indices. Consequently, some products in the Bybit TradFi product line (such as certain indices and crude oil) are affected. Please note: • Affected CME-linked products are currently unable to provide real-time quotes. • Opening, closing, and modifying orders for these products are temporarily unavailable. • Stop-loss and other risk control orders may not be triggered until market conditions resume. • XAUUSD (gold) is unaffected and can be traded normally. Bybit is closely monitoring the progress of the CME disruption. Once the CME resumes normal market operations, trading in related products will also resume simultaneously. The CME has not yet provided an estimated recovery time.

data.title: Chrome Extension Injects Hidden Fees Into Solana Swaps: New Report
data.description:

data.title: Bitcoin Faces Worst November Since 2019, Analysts Predict Positive Start to 2026
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, Bitcoin is poised to close November with its most significant loss since at least 2019, yet analysts suggest this downturn may set the stage for a promising start to 2026. LVRG research director Nick Ruck noted that while November will end negatively for the cryptocurrency, it presents an opportunity for astute investors to re-enter the market. He emphasized that the removal of overleveraged participants and unsustainable projects paves the way for new long-term holders to establish positions ahead of a potentially favorable new year.

Bitcoin (BTC) has declined nearly 16.9% this month, trading around $91,500, approaching losses from November 2019 when it fell by almost 17.3%, as reported by CoinGlass. The cryptocurrency's worst November occurred in 2018, with a 36.5% drop during the bear market following the 2017 peak. It last ended November in the red in 2022, with a 16.2% decrease. Crypto educator Sumit Kapoor remarked that November is typically one of Bitcoin's strongest months, but with the Thanksgiving weekend approaching, it is on track to be the worst November since 2018. He noted that historically, a red November often leads to a red December.

Justin d’Anethan, head of research at Arctic Digital, shared with Cointelegraph that crypto-native investors are accustomed to a somewhat predictable four-year cycle, which has previously led to rallies towards year-end, with October, November, and often December finishing positively. He attributed the early cycle trigger to the launch of spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds in the US in early 2024. D’Anethan views this development positively, suggesting it indicates a shift as institutions enter the market significantly, altering the pace and timing of crypto price movements.

Technical analysts are closely monitoring Bitcoin's monthly candle, predicting it may close above $93,000, with potential downside if momentum is not maintained over the weekend. Analyst "CrediBull Crypto" highlighted key levels to watch at the monthly close, specifically $93,401 and $102,437. They suggested a close above $93,000 would be a positive indicator, while a close above $102,000 would be extremely bullish, though it might require waiting until next month. At the time of writing, Bitcoin was trading at $91,600, remaining flat over the last 24 hours and struggling to break resistance just below $92,000.

data.title: Bitcoin Bullish Rebound Sparks Path Toward The $98,000 Imbalance Zone
data.description:
Bitcoin is showing new signs of strength after its sharp decline, with buyers stepping back in and momentum shifting upward. With price reclaiming key support levels, the path toward the major $98,000 imbalance zone is now back on the table, but bulls still need to prove this rebound has real conviction. FVG Filled, Bearish OB Tagged — What Comes After The Perfect Hit? Crypto analyst Crypto Patel, in a recent market update, noted that Bitcoin has now completed a key technical move by filling the Fair Value Gap (FVG) and tapping directly into the Bearish Order Block exactly as previously projected. He emphasized that traders who avoided shorting the $81,000–$85,000 region and instead positioned for the upside likely captured a clean and predictable long setup
source: https://www.newsbtc.com/news/bitcoin/bitcoin-sparks-path-to-98000/

data.title: Binance Offers Higher APR for ETH and SOL Staking Until End of 2025
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, the platform is set to enhance its staking offerings by introducing a limited-time higher Annual Percentage Rate (APR) for Ethereum (ETH) and Solana (SOL) staking. This initiative aims to reward the community and improve user experience, with the promotion running until the end of 2025. Users who subscribe to ETH and SOL staking by December 31, 2025, will benefit from increased APR rates, with ETH staking offering up to 2.6% APR and SOL staking providing up to 5.6% APR.

Binance has updated the Price Index Components for WBETH and BNSOL to ensure more reliable price tracking for liquidations. The index price will rely on official conversion ratios, minimizing the impact of token spot market volatility and accurately reflecting the underlying staked asset value. The Collateral Ratios for WBETH and BNSOL on Portfolio Margin and Cross Margin remain consistent with ETH and SOL respectively.

Binance ETH Staking (WBETH) allows users to stake Ethereum tokens to support the network's operations, contributing to its security and efficiency. Users receive WBETH tokens, representing staked ETH and rewards. Binance SOL Staking (BNSOL) offers a tradable form of staked SOL plus rewards, providing flexibility to sell, transfer, or use the staked position outside the Binance platform while still earning rewards. BNSOL accumulates staking rewards through the BNSOL:SOL conversion rate, even when used in other Binance products or external DeFi applications.

Terms and conditions apply to this activity, and users must adhere to Binance's guidelines. Binance reserves the right to adjust APRs and disqualify participants involved in dishonest behavior. The platform may amend terms without prior notice, and users are responsible for understanding regional restrictions on Binance services.

data.title: Chainlink Increases LINK Holdings by Over 89,000 Tokens
data.description: According to PANews, Chainlink has announced an update on its reserve data via the X platform. On November 27, Chainlink's reserves increased by 89,079.05 LINK tokens. As of this date, the total holdings amount to 973,752.70 LINK tokens, valued at approximately $12.89 million.
